name: Lowe Mill
content:
A former textile mill which was converted into an arts space. Lots of cool art studios, food trucks, and other things happening at Lowe Mill, as well as one of the few cycle shops in Huntsville They have a dog!. The space is personally bankrolled by the Hudson-Alpha family, although the caretakers of the space are allowed relatively free reign over how the money is used.
My first thought when I visited the neighborhood was that it was well into the first stages of gentrification. Three years later, a local man explained to me that if you look closely, you could start to see gentrification creeping into the neighborhood, to which I acted surprised out of politeness while thinking to myself “no shit Sherlock, there’s an entire block of brand new high end apartments across the street sticking out like a sore thumb.” Unfortunately, that is the way that life tends to go My friends from Chicago tell me it is possible to successfully legislate around this issue, but I will leave it to them to explain how it works.. Growing up in the San Francisco Bay Area made me a little sensitive to gentrification.
